Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

The decision that we collectively made is to use DLC as the LNet config front end. This solution does not go through DLC. I would rather have it be done through DLC, rather than the way it's done now

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 2:

DLC was conceived as a replacement to module parameters (make them changeable dynamically) and not a replacement to procfs or sysfs.  DLC has evolved to include some information from procfs (i.e. routing info, peer info), but that is a bonus and not the primary purpose of DLC.  

DLC is not replacing previous config systems but is "adding" to them.  There are cases like MultiRail which is new and would be too painful to do with the older config system so it will be DLC-only.

We have existing procfs files which are writable for resetting stats (i.e. /proc/sys/lnet/stats).  That has not been added to DLC yet.  It does not make sense to make this change DLC-only.  If that decision is made, then a new ticket should be created to collectively update all the LNet procfs files in one go rather than try to do them piecemeal.

parents.subject: LU-9234 test: Skip test_70f if OSS version is older than 2.9.53

commit.subject: LU-7214 lnet: Allow min stats to be reset in peers and nis
commit.message:
LU-7214 lnet: Allow min stats to be reset in peers and nis

Allow writes to the peers and nis LNet procfs files to
reset the mininum stat columns.

Signed-off-by: Doug Oucharek <doug.s.oucharek@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ic7aa647a154a424b35be1598266887d27efb4ab3
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/20470
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Olaf Weber <olaf@sgi.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Amir Shehata <amir.shehata@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
